channel.title: microarchitectural attacks — algoatson.github.io
channel.description: A bounds check the CPU hasn't finished evaluating doesn't stop it from speculatively running the code behind it. Spectre v1 trains the branch predictor to run an out-of-bounds read, encodes the secret into the cache, and reads it back with FLUSH+RELOAD after the architecture rolls everything else away.

item.title: FLUSH+RELOAD — reading secrets through the cache
item.description: You don't always need to read a victim's memory to learn its secrets — you can watch which lines it pulls into cache. Flush a shared line, let the victim run, time how long it takes to reload, and the access latency tells you whether the victim touched it. Repeat, and a secret-dependent access pattern falls out.

item.title: Prime+Probe — a side channel with nothing shared
item.description: FLUSH+RELOAD needs memory shared with the victim. Prime+Probe drops that requirement: fill a cache set with your own lines, let the victim run, and time how many of yours got evicted. You lose address-level resolution and gain the ability to spy across processes and VMs that share nothing but silicon.

item.title: Spectre v1 — the CPU runs code it shouldn't
item.description: A bounds check the CPU hasn't finished evaluating doesn't stop it from speculatively running the code behind it. Spectre v1 trains the branch predictor to run an out-of-bounds read, encodes the secret into the cache, and reads it back with FLUSH+RELOAD after the architecture rolls everything else away.
